title: 4 to 6 km Achilles Return Test Run

sections.markdown:
- 4 to 6 km easy return-to-run test only if the Achilles is no worse than trace soreness or stiffness on waking.
- Flat route only. No hills, strides, surges, marathon pace, or threshold work.
- Pace guide: start around `6:30-7:00 /km`; only let it settle toward `6:05-6:30 /km` if the tendon feels unchanged and the stride is normal.
- HR guardrail: keep this in Z1 to low Z2, ideally under about `150 bpm`.
- Run-walk is acceptable, for example `4 min` easy jog / `1 min` walk for `30-40 min`.
- Stop immediately if pain rises above mild awareness, changes your stride, gets sharper as you warm up, or lingers after stopping.
- If Thursday morning is not clearly improved, replace this with 30 to 45 minutes easy walking or rest.
